summary: Iteration 44 — Phase 3+4 + credential refresh backend testing complete. 44/45 pytest tests PASS (1 skipped due to no seeded enquiries). Verified: (a) linkage graph endpoint for clients/projects/vendors/purchase_orders with correct {resource, record_id, anchor, groups[]} shape & no _id leak, (b) Google Sheets channels list/create/delete with proper RBAC (super_admin/director/general_manager/accounts_executive for write, super_admin/director for delete) + 502 graceful failure on invalid CSV + 404 on unknown channel, (c) Tally config GET/PUT (super_admin only — 403 for project_manager), test endpoint returns {ok:false, error} 200 (no 500), sync-masters returns 502 when host unreachable & 400 when disabled, ledgers list/search supports empty results. All 13 demo accounts from /app/memory/test_credentials.md log in successfully. OneDrive regression (settings, queue, stats, test-connection, /api/uploads enqueue) all PASS.

minor.issue: No enquiries records currently seeded in DB — parametrized test for 'enquiries' resource was SKIPPED. Code path is identical to other resources (handled via LINK_MAP) and is exercised indirectly through clients.linkage->enquiries group, but a direct enquiry-as-anchor smoke would need at least one enquiry doc.

action_items: Optional: seed at least one enquiry record so the linkage graph enquiries-anchor path can be smoke-tested directly (currently only verified transitively via clients→enquiries group).

Optional: the Tally /test endpoint returns 200 with {ok:false,error}, while /sync-masters returns 502 on unreachable host. Both are reasonable but inconsistent — consider standardising (probably keep both as-is since /test is a diagnostic and /sync is a transactional op).
critical_code_review_comments: Good: LINK_MAP dict-driven design in linkage_router.py is clean and extensible (one-line addition for new collections). $field token substitution via _substitute() correctly skips missing source fields so partially-populated parents do not generate spurious empty queries.
Good: All Mongo queries use {'_id': 0} projection — no ObjectId leakage observed in any response (anchor doc or group items).

Good: RBAC enforced consistently — sheets write requires super_admin/director/general_manager/accounts_executive (verified 403 for project_manager); sheets delete tighter to super_admin/director; tally config/test/sync entirely super_admin (verified 403 for project_manager).

Good: External I/O is properly wrapped — CSV fetch and Tally XML POST both convert network exceptions to HTTP 502 (no 500/stacktrace leakage). httpx uses follow_redirects=True for sheets and short timeout (10s) for tally /test.
Good: Tally test endpoint persists last_test_at + last_test_ok + last_test_error on every call — observable on subsequent GET /tally/config.
Good: sheet_channels.fetch updates last_synced_at + last_row_count and caps response at 500 rows (good for huge sheets).

Minor: GET /linkage/graph could leak large sub-collection lists — currently capped at 50 per group via .limit(50). For projects with hundreds of POs that limit is silently truncated with no 'has_more' indicator. Consider returning total count from a separate count_documents call.

Minor: _normalise_sheet_url() coerces /edit URLs to /export?format=csv but the latter requires the doc to be viewable-by-link; otherwise returns HTML (which then fails CSV parse silently — empty data with HTML tags as a header). Consider validating Content-Type=text/csv before parsing and surfacing a clearer error.

Minor: linkage_router does not check tenant/site scoping — a project_manager can call /linkage/graph for any project (including ones outside their assigned sites). If RBAC on linkage data is required, add per-resource scope filters before returning anchor.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iteration44_linkage.py (new — 45 tests: 13 credential logins + 7 linkage graph + 9 sheets + 9 tally + 5 onedrive regression)
test_credentials: All 13 accounts in /app/memory/test_credentials.md now login with HTTP 200: admin@erp.com, test_pm@erp.com, sales@erp.com, hr.test@erp.com, purchase@erp.com, store@erp.com, safety@erp.com, supervisor@erp.com, director@erp.com, gm@erp.com, depthead.ops@erp.com, depthead.hse@erp.com, test_site_engineer@erp.com — re-seed verified.
seed_data_creation: Test transiently creates one sheet_channel ('TEST_iter44_channel') and deletes it within the same run. Tally settings doc persists company='TEST_ITER44' with enabled=false at end of run (super_admin can clear via PUT /linkage/tally/config). One file 'TEST_iter44_linkage.txt' uploaded via /api/uploads to verify onedrive_queue enqueue hook (remains in queue, harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All linkage_router endpoints fully verified. Test file is self-contained at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration44_linkage.py. Re-run: `cd /app && REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://worksite-command.preview.emergentagent.com pytest backend/tests/test_iteration44_linkage.py -v`. Iteration 43's test_iteration43_onedrive.py still passes (regression covered here too). Credentials file is now fully aligned with DB state — earlier iter43 finding about stale credentials is RESOLVED.
rca of the issue: No bugs found. All graceful-failure paths (invalid Google Sheet URL → 502; no Tally server reachable → 502 on sync, {ok:false} 200 on /test; disabled flag → 400 on sync; unknown channel → 404; unsupported resource → 404; missing record → 404) behave exactly as designed.
